region_of_origin: China; widely adopted in Singapore and Malaysia

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a steamed bun class (FOODON_03302050); fried mantou is a deep-fried variant. No exact FoodOn class for fried mantou exists; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar bakery products — wheat-based deep-fried bread
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated fried mantou entry. A00LC (wheat-based deep-fried bread) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for fried mantou or close equivalent in clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of fried mantou is one piece weighing approximately 60 g, providing around 207 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food — Fried Mantou / Deep-fried Steamed Bun
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo database is the primary regional source for Singaporean food items. Values for fried mantou are adapted from HPB data for deep-fried wheat-based steamed buns. Micronutrient values (potassium, magnesium, phosphorus, zinc, copper, manganese, selenium, vitamins) are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Bread, white, commercially prepared' (NDB 18069) adjusted for deep-frying fat absorption, where HPB does not report individual micronutrients. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 2.0 g per 100 g based on typical vegetable frying oil composition reported in USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Refined Wheat Products — Mantou and analogous deep-fried wheat bun
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined white flour mantou. Deep-frying adds fat which may marginally slow gastric emptying, but the overall GI remains high given the refined flour base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (75 × 27.9) / 100 = 20.9 g (carbohydrate per 60 g serving = 46.5 × 0.60 = 27.9 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation and typical consumption with high-sodium dipping sauces
hypertension.guidance: Fried mantou itself contains a moderate 120 mg sodium per 100 g, but its typical pairing with high-sodium sauces such as chili crab sauce substantially increases total sodium intake; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid high-sodium accompaniments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (75) and high refined carbohydrate content from white flour
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 75 and a glycemic load of approximately 20.9 per standard 60 g serving, fried mantou causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation and should be strictly limited or avoided by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying increases total fat and saturated fat content; refined flour contributes to atherogenic dietary pattern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-frying process raises total fat to approximately 14.8 g per 100 g with an estimated 2.0 g saturated fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and prefer steamed mantou as a lower-fat alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried foods and refined wheat may promote pro-inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Regular consumption of deep-fried refined wheat products may contribute to systemic inflammation; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis are advised to limit frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Mantou (炸馒头 / 炸饅頭; Zhà Mántou) is a deep-fried Chinese steamed bun that is especially popular in Singapore and Malaysia, where it is the classic accompaniment to Chili Crab and Black Pepper Crab. The bun is first prepared by steaming a leavened refined white wheat flour dough — producing the soft, pillowy interior characteristic of traditional mantou — and is then deep-fried in vegetable oil until a thin, golden-brown, and crispy outer crust forms. The result is a textural contrast between the crunchy exterior and the airy, chewy interior. Fried mantou is primarily used as a sauce vehicle, soaking up rich, savory seafood sauces, and is also served as a snack with sweetened condensed milk for dipping. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 345 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 14.8 g fat, and 46.5 g carbohydrate. The high refined carbohydrate content and high glycemic index (GI 75) make it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or following low-carbohydrate diets. The deep-frying process significantly increases caloric density and fat content compared to steamed mantou. It is a source of B vitamins including thiamine (0.12 mg) and niacin (1.1 mg) from the wheat flour base. Clinically, it is a high-GI, energy-dense food that should be consumed in moderation by individuals with diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension.
common_user_queries: How many calories in fried mantou?
What is the GI of mantou?
What do you eat with fried mantou?
Is fried mantou the same as steamed mantou?
meal_context: side dish at Chinese seafood restaurants

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved regional database for Singaporean food items, with micronutrient supplementation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. GI value is from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat mantou. The food is well-documented in the regional food composition literature.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat), cholesterol, and moisture data for deep-fried mantou are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18069) as of 2026-04-16; these fields cannot be reliably derived without specific laboratory analysis of the fried product. Saturated fat estimated at 2.0 g per 100 g based on typical vegetable frying oil composition from USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible or absent in refined wheat flour products per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 18069). The input record contained a duplicate portion entry (two identical '1 serving (60g)' entries); deduplicated to a single default serving of 60 g. Tamil alias 'தலா ஹுவா மந்தோ' is a transliteration of the Hindi phrase included for cross-language retrieval coverage; the primary Tamil alias 'வறுத்த மந்தோ' uses native Tamil vocabulary.
